mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2.5% annualized trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate hikes increase the discount rate applied to distant equity cash flows, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 200,000 monthly and a declining unemployment rate signal a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing capacity utilization signals supply-side efficiency gains that expand profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during supply-constrained expansions.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month for three consecutive months, contradicting the efficiency gain premise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect durable consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, which historically lead equity market appreciation over the subsequent 5 trading days through wealth effect transmission.
failure_condition: HOUST increases correlate with flat or negative SPX 5-day returns in more than 40% of observations across a 10-year backtest, breaking the wealth effect transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment crossing below the 5.5% threshold signals full labor market capacity, reducing recession fears and compressing equity risk premiums, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative or flat in over 50% of instances following the threshold breach, indicating the labor market signal has lost predictive power for equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable or falling consumer price index readings confirm disinflationary demand growth, which sustains corporate margin expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in more than 45% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, or CPIAUCSL decouples from RSAFS movements without equity market follow-through.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate increases and CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% annualized signal aggressive monetary tightening against persistent inflation, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as real rates spike.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite the dual signal of rising rates and elevated inflation in more than 50% of historical instances, invalidating the real rate transmission mechanism.
